behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the handoff boundary between file-loaded config, env overrides, and validation/error formatting. A unit test of only the validator could miss regressions where loader merge output or key-path propagation is wrong. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only mentions file+env merge and message content, not real network/subprocess/OS-specific behavior, and no AI/ML output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader through its real file+env merge path using a temp config file missing `database.host` and no matching `TOOL_...` env var; assert startup fails and emitted message contains exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This checks interaction between environment reading and path-resolution logic used by the loader. A pure unit of path join logic is close, but wiring regressions (loader ignoring resolver/env) are common and need module integration coverage. Rejected System: behavior is path selection, not subprocess/network or OS-event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, invoke loader startup path resolution, and assert the resolved/read path is `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml` (and not `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`).

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is precedence across modules: YAML load, env mapping (`TOOL_` -> key path), and merge order. Unit-testing merge function alone can miss mapping/wiring defects. Rejected higher levels: no real external infra beyond local process/env parsing, and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Provide config file with a key value and set conflicting `TOOL_...` env var; execute loader end-to-end (in-process modules) and assert resulting config uses env value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Needs integration between file read, YAML parser exception, and CLI error rendering that preserves parser line metadata. Unit-only formatting tests with mocked exceptions may hide real parser metadata shape changes. Rejected System: local temp-file parse is sufficient; no subprocess/network/OS-only behavior required.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML to a temp config file, run loader startup path, assert failure message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is cross-module startup orchestration plus local filesystem side effects (mkdir + default file write). Integration with temp filesystem is the minimum realistic boundary. Rejected System: behavior text does not require OS-event semantics, subprocesses, or external services; local filesystem in test process is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp HOME/XDG path where config dir is absent; run loader startup; assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader continues.
